task_completion.description: Verify the sample points layer is loaded into the GIS project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layers panel shows 'sample_points' loaded, and 3 points are visible on the map canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify the points are reprojected to EPSG:32610 for accurate distance measurement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A reprojected layer is visible in the layers panel, or a reprojection dialog shows the target CRS as EPSG:32610 (UTM Zone 10N).

task_completion.description: Verify the multi-ring buffer tool is configured with 3 rings and 500-meter intervals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Multi-ring buffer (constant distance)' dialog is open with 'Number of rings' set to 3 and 'Distance between rings' set to 500.

task_completion.description: Verify the multi-ring buffer has been successfully generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Concentric polygon rings (9 total features, 3 around each point) are visible on the map canvas, and a 'Multi-ring buffer' layer appears in the layers panel.

task_completion.description: Verify the final buffer layer is exported as a GeoJSON file to the correct path with EPSG:4326 CRS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Vector Layer as...' dialog shows format 'GeoJSON', file name ending in '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/evacuation_zones.geojson', and CRS set to 'EPSG:4326 - WGS 84', or a success message indicating the file was saved to this path.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the QGIS graphical interface to perform the operations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show QGIS application windows, toolbars, and processing dialogs being actively used, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Ensure the buffer was actually generated by the tool and not mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Processing Toolbox or Multi-ring buffer dialog is visible, and the log/history shows the algorithm execution.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent didn't use a text editor or Python script to bypass the GIS software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of text editors (like nano, vim, gedit, VSCode) or terminal-based Python scripts being used to generate the GeoJSON file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source file contains 3 points. The multi-ring buffer algorithm requires 3 rings at 500-meter intervals, which produces exactly 9 polygon features (3 points × 3 rings). The processing must use EPSG:32610 (UTM Zone 10N) for accurate metric distance calculations. The final output must be saved to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/evacuation_zones.geojson in EPSG:4326 (WGS84).
